frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Aajkal shaadi bhi business deal jaisi ho gayi hai. Ladke saari zindagi kamao, phir divorce ho toh alimony ke naam pe reverse dowry do — aur upar se 498A ka darr alag. Isliye bolta hoon, young men should be very careful, bahut si ladkiyon ko aadmi nahi, uski financial stability chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women broadly as financially exploitative and portrays marriage/divorce laws as tools women use against men. It encodes the 'reverse dowry/alimony' narrative, legal grievance around 498A misuse, and generalization about women being interested only in money.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
